nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Campania
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Mario, Angelo, Gino, Alfonso, Antonio, Biagio, Caesar, Dino, Falco, Sisto, Alessandra, Amerigo, Carlo, Cosmo.  Spanish Manuel, Jose, Jorge, Luis, Roberto, Agustin, Amado.

Italian and Spanish: metonymic occupational name for a bell-ringer or bell-maker, from campana ‘bell’ (abbreviated from the Latin phrase vasa campana ‘Campanian vessels’, since bells were first produced in Campania).

variants.name: Campanella
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Angelo, Salvatore, Santo, Sal, Rocco, Calogero, Cosmo, Domenic, Vito, Aldo, Annamarie, Antonio.

Italian: from a diminutive of campana ‘bell’, applied as a metonymic occupational name for a maker of small bells or handbells, or as a means of distinguishing one branch or member of a family called Campana from another.

nameMeanings.name: Hugo
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Latinized form of   Hugh  , used throughout the Middle Ages in official documents, and now enjoying a revival as a given name.

en.lifeSummary: Hugo de Campania was born in 1300, in York, Yorkshire, England, United Kingdom. He had at least 1 daughter with Alice Box.
